style_note: Father and son collborate on this laid-back reggae reflection.
A defiant calypso anthem from the 15-time winner of the Antigua and Barbuda Calypso Competition.

artist_name: David Hudson, Alan Dargin, Matthew Doyle, Mark Atkins
style_note: A deep, earthy, yet hypnotic rhythm featuring the digeridoo and other traditional Aboriginal instruments.
Raw but infectious - this is a slice of pure, traditional Bahamian Rake-n-Scrape.
A transcendental, rain-summoning incantation where the natural forces of Bangladeshi folk are given a modern facelift by the regional phenomenon that is the Coke Studio.
An anthem for female empowerment featuring the wine-and-wuk kinetic energy of Barbadian Ragga Soca (Soul of calypso).
At once haunting and joyful, Black Catbird blends Garifuna rhytms with contemporary electronica to evoke the Belizean rainforest.

artist_name: Hubert Smith and his Coral Islanders
style_note: The energetic street rhythms of Bermudan Gombey are smooth-talked into this suave calypso swing.
A defiant manifesto for female and black empowerment - full of edgy punk energy against a hip-hop foundation.
A tongue-in-cheek calypso defence of Elvis Presley, released at the height of 1950s Rock n Roll.

artist_name: Fauziah Gambus, Yunk-G, Dan.EL, KOEL & Whooguan
style_note: A vibrant reimagining of the Bruneian maritime folk song, fusing traditional stringed instruments with rap energy and hip-hop beats.
A joyous slice of 1970s Cameroonian disco funk layered on top of traditional Makossa grooves.
Elisapie, a singer from Salluit, Nunavut, translates the iconic Metallica song into her Inuktitut language, transforming it into a tender, poetic protest against colonisation.
A lively, foot-tapping example of Cayman 'kitchen dance music', in which everyday household utensils were used to bring the rhythm alongside the Celtic fiddle and cowskin drum.
The relentlessly high-energy rhythms of the Cook Island 'pate' (log drum), recorded in 1975 to mark the 10th anniversary of the Cook Islands' self-governance (Te Maeva Nui)
Monsieur Doumani keep traditional Cypriot music and instruments alive by giving them a modern twist, such as in this infectious, playful instrumental number.
